steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The first sentence makes a broad statement (Srinivasan worked at JPL). The second sentence narrows to a specific role (data scientist on the Perseverance rover team). This is a specification pattern.

steps.title: Apply the transition to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is specification: sentence 2 zooms in on the precise nature of the JPL work.
  • Choice A — Specifically,: signals a move to a more precise or particular detail of what was just stated. Fits: the rover role specifies what her JPL work entailed. ✓
  • Choice B — Conversely,: signals an opposite or contrasting situation. The rover role does not contrast with working at JPL. ✗
  • Choice C — Finally,: signals the last item in a sequence or the conclusion of a list. No prior sequence is established. ✗
  • Choice D — Nevertheless,: signals a concession despite prior conditions. No prior obstacle or contrast exists here. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
